name: Ride to Sierra Espuña — circular from Totana via Lorca
name_original: Rodeo a Sierra Espuña — circular desde Totana por Lorca
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Totana are primarily defined by the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Espuña Regional Park, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features deep pine and oak-forested valleys, rugged peaks, and a network of paved roads. Cyclists can expect routes with varying degrees of difficulty, including challenging ascents and mountain passes like Collado Bermejo. The area also includes unique geological formations such as the Badlands of Gebas. Best road cycling routes around Totana The most popular road cycling route is Morrón de Sierra Espuña Loop from Totana, a 31.6 miles (50.8 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 4 minutes to complete, leading through the rugged peaks and forested valleys of Sierra Espuña. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ride to Sierra Espuña — circular from Totana via Lorca, a difficult 63.0 miles (101.4 km) path that explores the varied terrain of the regional park. Local road cyclists also love the Espuña South Pass – Collado Bermejo loop from Totana, a 54.3 miles (87.4 km) trail leading through the pine forest and panoramic viewpoints of Collado Bermejo, often completed in about 5 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling around Totana is defined by the Sierra Espuña Regional Park, deep pine and oak-forested valleys, and challenging mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to routes requiring very good fitness. The routes in Totana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Totana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Totana?
A: Road cycling routes around Totana primarily feature the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Espuña Regional Park. You'll encounter deep pine and oak-forested valleys, rugged peaks, and a network of paved roads. The terrain varies from moderate rides with up to 500 feet of elevation gain to challenging ascents exceeding 1500 feet, some requiring very good fitness. The region also includes unique geological formations like the Badlands of Gebas.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Totana area?
A: There are over 70 road cycling routes available around Totana, catering to various skill levels. This includes 9 easy routes, 36 moderate routes, and 25 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Totana?
A: Yes, Totana offers 9 easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes provide a gentle introduction to the region's beautiful scenery without demanding significant fitness levels.

Q: What are some of the most scenic viewpoints or natural features to see while cycling in Totana?
A: The region boasts several stunning viewpoints and natural features. You can experience the panoramic views from &lt;strong&gt;Collado Bermejo&lt;/strong&gt;, located at 1,201 meters in Sierra Espuña, or the &lt;strong&gt;Virgen Blanca Viewpoint&lt;/strong&gt; near the Sanctuary of Santa Eulalia. The unique geological formations of the &lt;strong&gt;Badlands of Gebas&lt;/strong&gt; and the historical 'Pozos de la Nieve' (Snow Wells) are also notable sights.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes around Totana?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, Totana offers several options. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009726441/ride-to-sierra-espuna-circular-from-totana-via-lorca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ride to Sierra Espuña — circular from Totana via Lorca&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 101 kilometers and explores the varied terrain of the regional park.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Totana?
A: The road cycling routes in Totana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 40 reviews. Over 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the diverse terrain and the challenging ascents within the Sierra Espuña Regional Park.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions accessible by road bike near Totana?
A: Absolutely. You can cycle to the beautiful &lt;strong&gt;Sanctuary of Santa Eulalia (La Santa)&lt;/strong&gt;, a significant landmark with Tuscan architecture and a Mudéjar craft center, located about 7 kilometers from town. The &lt;strong&gt;La Bastida Archaeological Site&lt;/strong&gt;, one of Europe's most important archaeological discoveries, also offers a glimpse into the region's ancient history.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available from Totana?
A: Yes, many routes around Totana are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008605445/morron-de-sierra-espuna-loop-from-totana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morrón de Sierra Espuña Loop from Totana&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 50.8 km trail leading through the rugged peaks and forested valleys of Sierra Espuña.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Totana?
A: The Region of Murcia generally offers favorable conditions for outdoor activities. While specific data for road cycling seasons isn't provided, the diverse landscapes suggest that spring and autumn would be ideal for comfortable temperatures, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying clear skies.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Totana is well-suited for experienced riders with 25 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21853296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espuña South Pass – Collado Bermejo loop from Totana&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 87.4 km trail with significant elevation gain, leading through pine forests and panoramic viewpoints.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by castles or gorges near Totana?
A: Yes, some routes may take you near historical sites and natural wonders. For castles, you might find yourself close to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6012789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lorca Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5650752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Homage of Aledo&lt;/a&gt;. For gorges, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5650751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho de la Arboleja&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3037058" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de los Ballesteros – Lentisco Trail&lt;/a&gt; are notable natural features in the vicinity.
tips: Next to the junction of the 515 highway, we find these views that will accompany us during a good part of the route along the C-25 with impressive views.

Rolling along the highway of the Murcia region (RM-515) with some sections of asphalt in poor condition but which will be completely rehabilitated (in theory) during 2022. We will cross this hill at 465 meters of altitude which bears the name of the beautiful Sierra that will give us beautiful pictures along this road.

The C5 highway runs along the entire north face of Sierra Espuña, from Pliego passing through Casas Nuevas and connecting with the MU-503. It is an up and down road with little traffic surrounded, in spring, by green fields.
